task_completion.description: Open Blender and load the provided BMW car model.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Blender is open and the BMW car model is visible in the 3D viewport.

task_completion.description: Add at least three Area or Spot lights for a 3-point lighting setup.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Outliner panel shows at least three light objects added to the scene, specifically with icons indicating they are Area or Spot lights, or the viewport shows their distinct shapes.

task_completion.description: Set a dark studio world background.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The World Properties tab shows the background color set to a very dark shade or black, or the rendered viewport preview clearly shows a near-black background.

task_completion.description: Position the camera for a classic automotive 3/4 front view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The camera view or viewport shows the car from an angle that captures both the front fascia and one side of the vehicle simultaneously.

task_completion.description: Render the image and save it to /home/ga/BlenderProjects/product_shot.png.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender Render Result window is visible with the rendered car, or a file browser/terminal shows 'product_shot.png' successfully saved in the /home/ga/BlenderProjects/ directory.
The Blender window title bar displays 'studio_setup.blend' or a file browser/terminal shows the file saved in the /home/ga/BlenderProjects/ directory.

integrity.description: The agent actually used Blender to set up the scene and render.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Blender GUI (viewport, outliner, properties panels) or its internal scripting console, rather than just writing a standalone script in a text editor to bypass visual setup.

integrity.description: The rendered image matches the scene setup in Blender.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final render shown in the image viewer or render window visually corresponds to the lighting, camera angle, and background configured in the Blender viewport.

integrity.description: The agent manually configured the lights and camera rather than downloading a pre-made scene.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows the step-by-step addition of lights and adjustment of camera/world settings, rather than simply downloading and opening a completed .blend file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The source file is /home/ga/BlenderDemos/BMW27.blend. The expected outputs are /home/ga/BlenderProjects/studio_setup.blend and /home/ga/BlenderProjects/product_shot.png. The scene must contain at least 3 lights, and they must be of type AREA or SPOT. The camera height should be between 0.5m and 3.0m to achieve the automotive 3/4 front view. The world background brightness must be very dark (max 0.15). The final render must be a non-empty image (min 100KB).
